headline: Penan blockade enters 3rd year over logging roads
context: Penan communities in Sarawak's Baram district maintained blockades against logging roads for a third consecutive year in 2025, defending native customary land.

cards.big: Penan communities blockade Baram logging roads for 3rd year since 2023
cards.sub: 12 villages defend 50,000 hectares of ancestral forest from timber concessions

cards.big: Sarawak Land Code Section 5 recognises native customary rights pre-1958
cards.sub: Timber licences often overlap with NCR claims creating legal conflicts

cards.big: UN Declaration on Indigenous Peoples requires free prior informed consent
cards.sub: Malaysia voted in favour of UNDRIP in 2007 but implementation is minimal

cards.big: Penan population of 16,000 relies on forest for 70% of food and livelihood
cards.sub: Logging reduces water quality, game populations, and medicinal plant access

cards.big: When communities must physically blockade their own land, the system has failed
Sarawak government issued 14 new timber concessions in Baram since 2020
